revision.text: #redirect[[en:Yamana_Shrine_Tenno_Festival_Bugaku]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''山名神社天王祭舞楽'''（やまなじんじゃてんのうさいぶがく）は、[[静岡県]][[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]飯田に伝わる民俗芸能の[[舞楽]]。
[[1974年]][[12月4日]]付けで[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、[[1982年]][[1月14日]]には[[小国神社の舞楽]]および[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とともに、[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
{{japan-culture-stub}} [[category:静岡県|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:伝統芸能|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]]

[[1974年]][[12月4日]]付けで[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、[[1982年]][[1月14日]]には[[小国神社の舞楽]]および[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とともに、[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。

revision.text:
'''山名神社天王祭舞楽'''（やまなじんじゃてんのうさいぶがく）は、[[静岡県]][[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]飯田に伝わる民俗芸能の[[舞楽]]。
[[1974年]][[12月4日]]付けで[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、[[1982年]][[1月14日]]には[[小国神社の舞楽]]および[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とともに、[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
==関連項目== *[[山名神社]]
{{japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の祭り|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:森町_(静岡県)|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]]

'''山名神社天王祭舞楽'''（やまなじんじゃてんのうさいぶがく）は、[[静岡県]][[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]飯田に伝わる民俗芸能の[[舞楽]]。[[7月14日]]、15日に最も近い土日に行われる[[山名神社]]の祭典の折り境内の舞殿で奉納される。
各地の[[寺社]]に[[伝承]]されている[[稚児]]を主体とした舞楽の一つであるが、風流が舞楽化している点には他に例を見ない特徴がある。「八初児」「神子舞」「鶴」「獅子」「迦陵頻」「龍」「蟷螂」「優填獅子」の8曲が舞われるが、舞い手が鶴、竜、蟷螂などの作りものを頭にいただく姿、また「竜の舞」の途中、柱によじ登り逆さになりえびぞる動作をするなど、[[小国神社の舞楽]]や[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とは対照的な、正統舞楽とは趣きを異にした独特の特徴がある。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月4日]][[選択無形民俗文化財|記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]に選択され、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[1月14日]]には、小国神社の舞楽および天宮神社の十二段舞楽とともに[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
{{japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の祭り|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:森町_(静岡県)|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:雅楽|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]]

'''山名神社天王祭舞楽'''（やまなじんじゃてんのうさいぶがく）は、[[静岡県]][[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]飯田に伝わる民俗芸能の[[舞楽]]。[[7月14日]]、15日に最も近い土日に行われる[[山名神社]]の祭典の折り境内の舞殿で奉納される。
各地の[[寺社]]に[[伝承]]されている[[稚児]]を主体とした舞楽の一つであるが、風流が舞楽化している点には他に例を見ない特徴がある。「八初児」「神子舞」「鶴」「獅子」「迦陵頻」「龍」「蟷螂」「優填獅子」の8曲が舞われるが、舞い手が鶴、竜、蟷螂などの作りものを頭にいただく姿、また「竜の舞」の途中、柱によじ登り逆さになりえびぞる動作をするなど、[[小国神社の舞楽]]や[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とは対照的な、正統舞楽とは趣きを異にした独特の特徴がある。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月4日]][[選択無形民俗文化財|記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]に選択され、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[1月14日]]には、小国神社の舞楽および天宮神社の十二段舞楽とともに[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
{{japan-culture-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の祭り|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:無形民俗文化財|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:森町_(静岡県)|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]] [[Category:雅楽|やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく]]

'''山名神社天王祭舞楽'''（やまなじんじゃてんのうさいぶがく）は、[[静岡県]][[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]飯田に伝わる民俗芸能の[[舞楽]]。[[7月14日]]、15日に最も近い土日に行われる[[山名神社]]の[[祭典]]（[[天王祭]]）の折り、境内の舞殿で奉納される。
== 概要 == 各地の[[寺社]]に[[伝承]]されている[[稚児]]を主体とした舞楽の一つであるが、[[風流踊|風流]]が舞楽化している点には他に例を見ない特徴がある。「八初児」「神子舞」「鶴」「獅子」「迦陵頻」「龍」「蟷螂」「優填獅子」の8曲が舞われるが、舞い手が鶴、竜、蟷螂などの作りものを頭にいただく姿、また「竜の舞」の途中、柱によじ登り逆さになりえびぞる動作をするなど、[[小国神社の舞楽]]や[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とは対照的な、正統舞楽とは趣きを異にした独特の特徴がある。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月4日]][[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]に選択され、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[1月14日]]には、小国神社の舞楽および天宮神社の十二段舞楽とともに[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
{{japan-culture-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく}} [[Category:静岡県の祭り]] [[Category:静岡県森町の歴史]] [[Category:雅楽]] [[Category:日本国選択無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:祇園祭・天王祭]]

[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月4日]][[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]に選択され、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[1月14日]]には、小国神社の舞楽および天宮神社の十二段舞楽とともに[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。

revision.text: [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%90%8D%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E7%A5%AD%E8%88%9E%E6%A5%BD]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]]

[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%90%8D%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E7%A5%AD%E8%88%9E%E6%A5%BD]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] '''Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku is a folk art''' form handed down in Iida , Morimachi, Shuchi District , Shizuoka Prefecture . It is dedicated in the dance hall on the grounds of Yamana Shrine during the festival ( Tenno Festival ) held on the weekend closest to July 14th and 15th .
== overview == [ Edit source ] It is one of the Bugaku dances performed mainly by young children and handed down at temples and shrines around the country , but it has a unique feature in that it has been transformed into a dance of elegance . It consists of eight pieces: "Yatsuhatsuko," "Mikomai," "Tsuru," "Shishi," "Karyobin," "Ryu," "Praying Mantis," and "Yutenshishi." The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .
On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine .

[[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E5%90%8D%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%A4%A9%E7%8E%8B%E7%A5%AD%E8%88%9E%E6%A5%BD]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] '''Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku is a folk art''' form handed down in Iida , Morimachi, Shuchi District , Shizuoka Prefecture . It is dedicated in the dance hall on the grounds of Yamana Shrine during the festival ( Tenno Festival ) held on the weekend closest to July 14th and 15th .
== overview == [ Edit source ] It is one of the Bugaku dances performed mainly by young children and handed down at temples and shrines around the country , but it has a unique feature in that it has been transformed into a dance of elegance . It consists of eight pieces: "Yatsuhatsuko," "Mikomai," "Tsuru," "Shishi," "Karyobin," "Ryu," "Praying Mantis," and "Yutenshishi." The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .
On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine .

[[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku}}[[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku}}[[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[en:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[en:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[en:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]]'''Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku is a folk art''' form handed down in Iida , Morimachi, Shuchi District , Shizuoka Prefecture . It is dedicated in the dance hall on the grounds of Yamana Shrine during the festival ( Tenno Festival ) held on the weekend closest to July 14th and 15th .
== overview == [ Edit source ] It is one of the Bugaku dances performed mainly by young children and handed down at temples and shrines around the country , but it has a unique feature in that it has been transformed into a dance of elegance . It consists of eight pieces: "Yatsuhatsuko," "Mikomai," "Tsuru," "Shishi," "Karyobin," "Ryu," "Praying Mantis," and "Yutenshishi." The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .
On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine . [[sn:amana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]] [[Category:Pages to be written 2]][[sn:Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku]][[Category:Pages to be written 2]]

The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .
On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine . On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine . 16 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:山名神社天王祭舞楽 up to rev 93622023
revision.text:
'''山名神社天王祭舞楽'''（やまなじんじゃてんのうさいぶがく）は、[[静岡県]][[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]飯田に伝わる民俗芸能の[[舞楽]]。[[7月14日]]、15日に最も近い土日に行われる[[山名神社]]の[[祭典]]（[[天王祭]]）の折り、境内の舞殿で奉納される。
== 概要 == 各地の[[寺社]]に[[伝承]]されている[[稚児]]を主体とした舞楽の一つであるが、[[風流踊|風流]]が舞楽化している点には他に例を見ない特徴がある。「八初児」「神子舞」「鶴」「獅子」「迦陵頻」「龍」「蟷螂」「優填獅子」の8曲が舞われるが、舞い手が鶴、竜、蟷螂などの作りものを頭にいただく姿、また「竜の舞」の途中、柱によじ登り逆さになりえびぞる動作をするなど、[[小国神社の舞楽]]や[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とは対照的な、正統舞楽とは趣きを異にした独特の特徴がある。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月4日]][[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]に選択され、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[1月14日]]には、小国神社の舞楽および天宮神社の十二段舞楽とともに[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
{{japan-culture-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまなしんしやてんのうさいふかく}} [[Category:静岡県の祭り]] [[Category:静岡県森町の歴史]] [[Category:雅楽]] [[Category:記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] [[Category:祇園祭・天王祭]]

== 概要 == 各地の[[寺社]]に[[伝承]]されている[[稚児]]を主体とした舞楽の一つであるが、[[風流踊|風流]]が舞楽化している点には他に例を見ない特徴がある。「八初児」「神子舞」「鶴」「獅子」「迦陵頻」「龍」「蟷螂」「優填獅子」の8曲が舞われるが、舞い手が鶴、竜、蟷螂などの作りものを頭にいただく姿、また「竜の舞」の途中、柱によじ登り逆さになりえびぞる動作をするなど、[[小国神社の舞楽]]や[[天宮神社の十二段舞楽]]とは対照的な、正統舞楽とは趣きを異にした独特の特徴がある。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）[[12月4日]][[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]に選択され、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）[[1月14日]]には、小国神社の舞楽および天宮神社の十二段舞楽とともに[[遠江森町の舞楽]]として[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。

'''Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku is a folk art''' form handed down in Iida , Morimachi, Shuchi District , Shizuoka Prefecture . It is dedicated in the dance hall on the grounds of Yamana Shrine during the festival ( Tenno Festival ) held on the weekend closest to July 14th and 15th .

== Overview ==
It is one of the Bugaku dances performed mainly by young children and handed down at temples and shrines around the country , but it has a unique feature in that it has been transformed into a dance of elegance . It consists of eight pieces: "Yatsuhatsuko," "Mikomai," "Tsuru," "Shishi," "Karyobin," "Ryu," "Praying Mantis," and "Yutenshishi." The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .

On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine .

[[Category:completion tier 1]]
{{translated page|ja|山名神社天王祭舞楽|version=93622023|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .
On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine .

'''Yamana Shrine Tenno Festival Bugaku is a folk art''' form handed down in Iida , Morimachi, Shuchi District , Shizuoka Prefecture . It is dedicated in the dance hall on the grounds of Yamana Shrine during the festival ( Tenno Festival ) held on the weekend closest to July 14th and 15th .
== Overview == It is one of the Bugaku dances performed mainly by young children and handed down at temples and shrines around the country , but it has a unique feature in that it has been transformed into a dance of elegance . It consists of eight pieces: "Yatsuhatsuko," "Mikomai," "Tsuru," "Shishi," "Karyobin," "Ryu," "Praying Mantis," and "Yutenshishi." The dancers wear artificial objects such as cranes, dragons, and praying mantises on their heads, and during the "Dragon Dance," they climb up a pillar and bend over upside down, giving it a unique style that is different from orthodox Bugaku and contrasts with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amamiya Shrine .
On December 4, 1974 , it was selected as an intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record keeping should be taken , and on January 14, 1982 , it was designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property as the Bugaku of Morimachi , Totomi , along with the Bugaku of Oguni Shrine and the Twelve-Step Bugaku of Amanomiya Shrine . {{translated page|ja|山名神社天王祭舞楽|version=93622023|comment=Imported full ja history}}
